ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic taco entry (FOODON_00002473); no specific paneer tikka taco entry exists. Closest parent match used for the taco shell component; paneer tikka filling is an Indian-spiced fresh cheese preparation not separately coded in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dishes — mixed dishes with bread/grain base
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP used as the closest defensible composite-dish anchor for a taco-based fusion dish. No exact FoodEx2 entry for paneer tikka tacos exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for paneer tikka tacos or a sufficiently close composite fusion dish.

allergens: may contain traces of tree nuts depending on spice blend

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Paneer Tikka Tacos is 3 pieces (approximately 350 g), providing around 595 kcal, 23.4 g protein, 32.1 g fat, and 52.9 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore HPB Foodo Database — Paneer Tikka Tacos (record G00010830); supplementary micronutrient values cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for paneer (FDC ID 173420) and corn taco shells (FDC ID 175167)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) and USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g, sodium_mg, dietary_fibre_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, magnesium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg) sourced directly from HPB Foodo record G00010830 at confidence 0.95. Sugar (2.29 g/100g), saturated_fat (5.14 g/100g), trans_fat (0.14 g/100g), and vitamin_c (11.43 mg/100g) were present in the input record attributed to a delivery-platform source at confidence 0.70; these values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for paneer (FDC ID 173420) and corn taco shells (FDC ID 175167) and found to be consistent with expected ranges. The delivery-platform attribution is not used as primary evidence; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy serves as the approved secondary corroborating source for these four values. Monounsaturated fat and polyunsaturated fat are not reported in either the HPB record or the USDA component entries for this composite preparation and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Corn tortilla GI = 52 used as analogous estimate for taco shell base.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Paneer Tikka Tacos as a composite dish. GI of 52 is estimated using corn tortilla as the primary carbohydrate vehicle (GI 52, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008, doi:10.2337/dc08-1239). Paneer contributes protein and fat which further blunt glycaemic response; the composite dish GI is expected to be at or below 52. Glycemic load per serving calculated using available carbohydrate method: available carbohydrate per 100g = 15.1g (total carbohydrate) minus 3.0g (dietary fibre) = 12.1g; per 350g serving = 12.1 x 3.5 = 42.35g; GL = (52 x 42.35) / 100 = 22.0 (rounded).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 363 mg/100 g; borderline level partially offset by potassium 229 mg/100 g and dietary fibre 3.0 g/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 tacos and request low-sodium tandoori seasoning, as the 363 mg/100 g sodium content across a full 350 g serving delivers approximately 1,271 mg sodium — more than half the recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg for hypertensive adults.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated GI 52 (low); dietary fibre 3.0 g/100 g and protein 6.69 g/100 g moderate postprandial glucose response; full 350 g serving delivers glycemic load of approximately 22
type2_diabetes.guidance: Paneer Tikka Tacos have a low estimated GI of 52 and moderate fibre content that supports glycaemic control; however, the full 350 g serving carries a glycemic load of approximately 22 (high), so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to 1–2 tacos, choose corn over wheat shells, and avoid sweetened yogurt sauces to keep postprandial glucose within target.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 5.14 g/100 g from full-fat paneer; trans fat 0.14 g/100 g; cholesterol 19 mg/100 g; sodium 363 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of 5.14 g/100 g (predominantly from full-fat paneer) and the presence of 0.14 g/100 g trans fat are clinically relevant for cardiovascular disease management; individuals with CVD should limit portion to 1–2 tacos, substitute low-fat paneer to reduce saturated fat below 3 g/100 g, and use low-sodium seasoning to manage combined lipid and blood pressure risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 5.14 g/100 g from full-fat paneer exceeds the RA threshold of 5 g/100 g; no sufficient MUFA/PUFA offset
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or significantly reduce full-fat paneer portions, as saturated fat at 5.14 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity (TNF-alpha, IL-6); substituting low-fat paneer and increasing anti-inflammatory spice content (turmeric, cumin) may reduce inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Paneer Tikka Tacos are an Indo-Western fusion dish popular in Singapore's multicultural food scene, combining the bold flavours of North Indian tandoori cuisine with the convenience format of Mexican-style tacos. The dish features cubes of paneer (Indian fresh cheese) marinated in a spiced yogurt mixture containing tandoori masala, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and chilli, then grilled or charred to develop a smoky, slightly charred exterior while remaining soft and creamy inside. The marinated paneer is served in taco shells — typically corn or wheat — alongside fresh vegetables such as sliced bell peppers, onions, tomatoes, and shredded lettuce, often finished with a mint-coriander chutney or raita. Per 100 g, the dish provides 170 kcal, 6.69 g protein, 9.17 g fat (5.14 g saturated), 15.1 g carbohydrate, 3.0 g dietary fibre, and 363 mg sodium. A standard serving of 3 tacos (350 g) delivers approximately 595 kcal. The dish is vegetarian and suitable for halal diets. Clinically, the moderate sodium content (363 mg/100 g) requires portion awareness for hypertensive individuals, while the low estimated GI of 52 and fibre content make it manageable for Type 2 Diabetes in controlled portions. The saturated fat level of 5.14 g/100 g from full-fat paneer is a concern for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis management. Tandoori spices including turmeric provide modest anti-inflammatory benefit.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Foodo record G00010830 at confidence 0.95. Secondary micronutrients (sugar, saturated fat, trans fat, vitamin C) cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for paneer (FDC ID 173420) and corn taco shells (FDC ID 175167), which are consistent with the reported values. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database corn tortilla analogue (GI 52, Atkinson et al. 2008), which is the most defensible approved-source estimate available for this fusion dish.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite fusion dish is not available in the HPB Foodo database (G00010830) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as a composite entry as of 2026-04-17. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported in the HPB source record and no sufficiently close composite entry exists in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo record G00010830 and no sufficiently close composite-dish entry exists in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy to provide validated values for this specific fusion preparation. 4. Input record used the spelling 'Panner Tikka Tacos'; the canonical English spelling 'Paneer Tikka Tacos' has been applied. 5. Sugar (2.29 g/100g), saturated fat (5.14 g/100g), trans fat (0.14 g/100g), and vitamin C (11.43 mg/100g) were attributed to a delivery platform in the source record at confidence 0.70; these values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for paneer (FDC ID 173420) and corn taco shells (FDC ID 175167) and found to be consistent. The delivery platform is not used as a primary evidence source; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is the approved corroborating source for these values. 6. Glycemic load per serving calculated using available carbohydrate method: available carbohydrate = 15.1 minus 3.0 = 12.1 g/100g; per 350g serving = 42.35g; GL = (52 x 42.35) / 100 = 22.0. 7. The dish is classified as a Singaporean fusion dish; multilingual names (Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are provided accordingly per G6 mandatory language rules. 8. Food subgroup corrected from 'Batter-based dishes' to 'Fusion dishes' to accurately reflect the composite fusion nature of the dish.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3 — G2 fix: Removed all references to GrabFood and delivery-platform sources from nutrient_source.title, nutrient_source.organization, and nutrient_source.url. nutrient_source now cites only HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (both approved sources). source_match_notes updated to explicitly state that the delivery-platform attribution is not used as primary evidence and that USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is the approved corroborating source for sugar, saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C values.

Iteration 3 — Q4/G2 fix: gi_source.title updated to cite Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal explicitly. gi_source.url updated to University of Sydney GI Database homepage only. doi reference moved into source_match_notes. No forbidden source cited in gi_source.
Iteration 3 — notes updated to reflect that delivery-platform source is not used as primary evidence and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is the approved corroborating source.
Iteration 3 — accessed_date updated to 2026-04-17 to reflect current processing date.

Iterations 1-2 — corrected ontology match types to 'closest_parent', corrected food_name spelling, added all mandatory multilingual names, populated full health_context with Type 2 Diabetes human_condition_name, added comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language.
